Xiaomi MiMo Code vs OVH AI Endpoints

Compare Xiaomi MiMo Code and OVH AI Endpoints: free credits, features, and ratings. Which one is right for you?

Xiaomi MiMo Code

Xiaomi's AI coding agent forked from OpenCode, open-sourced under MIT. The killer feature is the built-in MiMo Auto channel — zero config, free for 1 month, runs MiMo-V2.5 with 1M token context. Coding performance is on par with Claude Sonnet 4.6 on SWE-Bench, but 40-60% more token-efficient. Highlights: cross-session memory, voice input, auto knowledge distillation. After the free period, you need a Token Plan subscription.

Pros

  • ✓Free for 1 month, zero config
  • ✓Coding on par with Claude Sonnet 4.6
  • ✓40-60% more token-efficient than Claude
  • ✓1M token context window
  • ✓Persistent cross-session memory
  • ✓Voice input and auto knowledge distillation
  • ✓MIT open source, BYOK supported

Cons

  • ✗Free for only 1 month
  • ✗Free channel only gives V2.5, Pro requires payment
  • ✗Credit multiplier is high — advertised 1.6B credits sounds more than it is
  • ✗Ecosystem still early, community building
  • ✗Real-name auth required for paid plans

Key Features

MiMo Auto zero-config free channelCross-session persistent memory (SQLite FTS5)Compose mode for spec-driven developmentVoice input (TenVAD + MiMo ASR)/dream auto knowledge extraction + /distill skill miningGoal stop-condition verificationSubagent parallel system1M token context windowMIT open source
View Details →

OVH AI Endpoints

AI inference endpoints from French cloud veteran OVHcloud. Free tier: 12 RPM with multiple open-source models. European data centers for GDPR compliance.

Pros

  • ✓Established cloud provider with good stability
  • ✓European data centers
  • ✓GDPR compliant

Cons

  • ✗12 RPM is very low
  • ✗Requires VPN from China
  • ✗Limited model selection

Key Features

12 RPMOpen-Source ModelsEuropean Data Centers
View Details →
